output:
# solution.py
"""
Multi-Agent Quest Creator
=========================

A collaborative, real-time system that lets multiple role-playing game (RPG)
players design, balance, test, and share quests together.

The whole system lives in this single module. It is organised into clearly
separated logical layers (you can read each section as if it were its own file):

    1. Enumerations & primitives
    2. Domain models          -> Player / Enemy / Reward / Objective / NPC / Item
    3. Quest aggregate        -> the editable document players collaborate on
    4. Event bus              -> observer pattern powering *real-time* updates
    5. History (memento)      -> change tracking + revert to previous versions
    6. Balance engine         -> live difficulty/fairness analysis + suggestions
    7. Adaptation engine      -> preference-aware modification recommendations
    8. Quest simulator        -> "testing mode" that simulates a play-through
    9. Community hub          -> publish / rate / review / download quests
   10. Collaboration session  -> ties everything together for live co-editing
   11. QuestCreatorSystem     -> public facade (player accounts + sessions)
   12. Demo + self tests

Requirement coverage
---------------------
* Real-time multi-player collaboration ........ CollaborationSession + EventBus
* Real-time balance feedback .................. BalanceEngine (auto-broadcast)
* Adapt to feedback / suggest modifications ... AdaptationEngine + auto_balance
* History + revert ............................ QuestHistory (memento)
* Testing / simulation mode ................... QuestSimulator
* Quest types + custom content ................ QuestType + NPC / Item
* Community sharing, rating, reviewing ........ CommunityHub
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import math
import random
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Enumerations & primitives
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class QuestType(Enum):
    COMBAT = "combat"
    PUZZLE = "puzzle"
    EXPLORATION = "exploration"
    MIXED = "mixed"


class EnemyType(Enum):
    MELEE = "melee"
    RANGED = "ranged"
    MAGIC = "magic"
    BEAST = "beast"
    BOSS = "boss"


class RewardType(Enum):
    GOLD = "gold"
    EXPERIENCE = "experience"
    ITEM = "item"
    REPUTATION = "reputation"


class ObjectiveType(Enum):
    DEFEAT = "defeat"
    SURVIVE = "survive"
    SOLVE = "solve"
    COLLECT = "collect"
    EXPLORE = "explore"
    ESCORT = "escort"


class DifficultyTier(Enum):
    TRIVIAL = "Trivial"
    EASY = "Easy"
    BALANCED = "Balanced"
    HARD = "Hard"
    EXTREME = "Extreme"


def _new_id(prefix: str) -> str:
    """Generate a short, human-readable unique identifier."""
    return f"{prefix}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"


def _clamp(value: float, low: float, high: float) -> float:
    return max(low, min(high, value))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Domain models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Player:
    """A participant who can log in and collaborate on quests.

    ``preferences`` is a free-form dict understood by the AdaptationEngine.
    Recognised keys: ``preferred_ratio`` (float target challenge/power ratio),
    ``enemy_density`` ("low"/"high"), ``reward_focus`` (a RewardType value).
    """

    name: str
    combat_skill: float = 10.0
    puzzle_skill: float = 10.0
    exploration_skill: float = 10.0
    preferences: Dict[str, object] = field(default_factory=dict)
    player_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: _new_id("player"))
    online: bool = False

    def effective_skill(self, quest_type: QuestType) -> float:
        """How effective this player is at a given style of quest."""
        if quest_type == QuestType.COMBAT:
            return self.combat_skill
        if quest_type == QuestType.PUZZLE:
            return self.puzzle_skill
        if quest_type == QuestType.EXPLORATION:
            return self.exploration_skill
        return (self.combat_skill + self.puzzle_skill + self.exploration_skill) / 3.0


@dataclass
class Enemy:
    name: str
    enemy_type: EnemyType = EnemyType.MELEE
    strength: float = 5.0
    count: int = 1

    @property
    def total_strength(self) -> float:
        return self.strength * self.count


@dataclass
class Reward:
    name: str
    reward_type: RewardType = RewardType.GOLD
    value: float = 10.0


@dataclass
class Objective:
    description: str
    objective_type: ObjectiveType = ObjectiveType.DEFEAT
    difficulty_weight: float = 5.0
    optional: bool = False


@dataclass
class NPC:
    """Custom, player-created non-player character."""

    name: str
    role: str = "neutral"  # e.g. "ally", "healer", "guide", "enemy", "neutral"
    dialogue: str = ""
    created_by: Optional[str] = None
    npc_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: _new_id("npc"))


@dataclass
class Item:
    """Custom, player-created item. Positive ``power`` aids the party."""

    name: str
    description: str = ""
    power: float = 0.0
    created_by: Optional[str] = None
    item_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: _new_id("item"))


@dataclass
class Review:
    reviewer: str
    rating: int  # 1..5
    comment: str = ""
    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.now)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Quest aggregate
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Quest:
    """The collaborative document. Holds objectives, enemies, rewards and
    custom content, and knows how to mutate and snapshot itself."""

    title: str
    quest_type: QuestType = QuestType.COMBAT
    description: str = ""
    objectives: List[Objective] = field(default_factory=list)
    enemies: List[Enemy] = field(default_factory=list)
    rewards: List[Reward] = field(default_factory=list)
    npcs: List[NPC] = field(default_factory=list)
    items: List[Item] = field(default_factory=list)
    quest_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: _new_id("quest"))

    # ---- derived metrics -------------------------------------------------
    def enemy_count(self) -> int:
        return sum(e.count for e in self.enemies)

    def total_enemy_strength(self) -> float:
        return sum(e.total_strength for e in self.enemies)

    def total_objective_weight(self) -> float:
        required = sum(o.difficulty_weight for o in self.objectives if not o.optional)
        optional = sum(o.difficulty_weight * 0.5 for o in self.objectives if o.optional)
        return required + optional

    def total_reward_value(self) -> float:
        return sum(r.value for r in self.rewards)

    def support_power(self) -> float:
        """Aid provided to the party by friendly NPCs and beneficial items."""
        npc_aid = sum(3.0 for n in self.npcs if n.role in ("ally", "healer", "guide"))
        item_aid = sum(max(0.0, it.power) for it in self.items)
        return npc_aid + item_aid

    # ---- mutation operations --------------------------------------------
    def add_objective(self, objective: Objective) -> None:
        self.objectives.append(objective)

    def add_enemy(self, enemy: Enemy) -> None:
        self.enemies.append(enemy)

    def add_reward(self, reward: Reward) -> None:
        self.rewards.append(reward)

    def add_npc(self, npc: NPC) -> None:
        self.npcs.append(npc)

    def add_item(self, item: Item) -> None:
        self.items.append(item)

    def increase_enemy_count(self, amount: int = 1) -> None:
        """Add ``amount`` enemy units, reinforcing the strongest group."""
        if amount <= 0:
            return
        if not self.enemies:
            self.enemies.append(Enemy(name="Reinforcement", strength=5.0, count=amount))
            return
        target = max(self.enemies, key=lambda e: e.strength)
        target.count += amount

    def decrease_enemy_count(self, amount: int = 1) -> None:
        """Remove ``amount`` enemy units, thinning the weakest groups first."""
        for _ in range(max(0, amount)):
            if not self.enemies:
                return
            weakest = min(self.enemies, key=lambda e: e.strength)
            if weakest.count > 1:
                weakest.count -= 1
            else:
                self.enemies.remove(weakest)

    def scale_enemy_strength(self, factor: float) -> None:
        for e in self.enemies:
            e.strength = round(e.strength * factor, 2)

    def adjust_reward_value(self, factor: float) -> None:
        for r in self.rewards:
            r.value = round(r.value * factor, 2)

    def consolidate_enemies(self) -> None:
        """Merge many weak foes into fewer, stronger "elite" enemies while
        roughly preserving the total challenge."""
        if not self.enemies:
            return
        total = self.total_enemy_strength()
        target_count = max(1, self.enemy_count() // 2)
        archetype = max(self.enemies, key=lambda e: e.strength)
        self.enemies = [
            Enemy(
                name=f"Elite {archetype.name}",
                enemy_type=archetype.enemy_type,
                strength=round(total / target_count, 2),
                count=target_count,
            )
        ]

    # ---- snapshot / restore (for history) -------------------------------
    def snapshot(self) -> "Quest":
        return copy.deepcopy(self)

    def restore_from(self, other: "Quest") -> None:
        """Copy another quest's state in-place so external references stay valid."""
        self.title = other.title
        self.quest_type = other.quest_type
        self.description = other.description
        self.objectives = copy.deepcopy(other.objectives)
        self.enemies = copy.deepcopy(other.enemies)
        self.rewards = copy.deepcopy(other.rewards)
        self.npcs = copy.deepcopy(other.npcs)
        self.items = copy.deepcopy(other.items)

    # ---- presentation ----------------------------------------------------
    def describe(self) -> str:
        lines = [f"Quest: {self.title} [{self.quest_type.value}]"]
        if self.description:
            lines.append(f"  {self.description}")
        lines.append(f"  Objectives ({len(self.objectives)}):")
        for o in self.objectives:
            flag = " (optional)" if o.optional else ""
            lines.append(
                f"    - {o.description} [{o.objective_type.value}, w={o.difficulty_weight}]{flag}"
            )
        lines.append(f"  Enemies ({self.enemy_count()} units):")
        for e in self.enemies:
            lines.append(
                f"    - {e.name} x{e.count} [{e.enemy_type.value}, str={e.strength}]"
            )
        lines.append(f"  Rewards (value={self.total_reward_value():.0f}):")
        for r in self.rewards:
            lines.append(f"    - {r.name} [{r.reward_type.value}, {r.value}]")
        if self.npcs:
            lines.append("  Custom NPCs: " + ", ".join(f"{n.name}({n.role})" for n in self.npcs))
        if self.items:
            lines.append("  Custom Items: " + ", ".join(f"{it.name}(power={it.power})" for it in self.items))
        return "\n".join(lines)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Event bus (observer pattern -> real-time updates)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Event:
    type: str
    actor: str
    message: str
    payload: Dict[str, object] = field(default_factory=dict)
    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.now)


class EventBus:
    """Minimal publish/subscribe bus. Subscribers are notified synchronously,
    which models pushing live updates to every connected player's client."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._subscribers: List[Callable[[Event], None]] = []
        self.log: List[Event] = []

    def subscribe(self, callback: Callable[[Event], None]) -> Callable[[Event], None]:
        self._subscribers.append(callback)
        return callback

    def unsubscribe(self, callback: Callable[[Event], None]) -> None:
        if callback in self._subscribers:
            self._subscribers.remove(callback)

    def publish(self, event: Event) -> None:
        self.log.append(event)
        for callback in list(self._subscribers):
            callback(event)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. History (memento pattern -> change tracking + revert)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class HistoryEntry:
    version: int
    author: str
    message: str
    snapshot: Quest
    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.now)


class QuestHistory:
    """Append-only log of quest snapshots supporting revert to any version."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.entries: List[HistoryEntry] = []

    @property
    def current_version(self) -> int:
        return self.entries[-1].version if self.entries else 0

    def commit(self, quest: Quest, author: str, message: str) -> int:
        version = self.current_version + 1
        self.entries.append(HistoryEntry(version, author, message, quest.snapshot()))
        return version

    def find(self, version: int) -> Optional[HistoryEntry]:
        for entry in self.entries:
            if entry.version == version:
                return entry
        return None

    def revert(self, quest: Quest, version: int, author: str = "system") -> int:
        entry = self.find(version)
        if entry is None:
            raise ValueError(f"No such version: {version}")
        quest.restore_from(entry.snapshot)
        # Append-only: the revert itself becomes a new version.
        return self.commit(quest, author, f"Reverted to v{version}")

    def log(self) -> List[str]:
        return [
            f"v{e.version} | {e.author}: {e.message} ({e.timestamp:%H:%M:%S})"
            for e in self.entries
        ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Balance engine (live difficulty/fairness analysis)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Suggestion:
    action: str
    description: str
    params: Dict[str, object] = field(default_factory=dict)
    rationale: str = ""


@dataclass
class BalanceReport:
    party_power: float
    challenge_rating: float
    ratio: float
    score: float  # 0..100, where 100 means perfectly balanced
    tier: DifficultyTier
    reward_balance: float  # actual / expected reward value
    suggestions: List[Suggestion] = field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def is_balanced(self) -> bool:
        return self.tier == DifficultyTier.BALANCED

    def summary(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"Difficulty: {self.tier.value} | balance {self.score:.0f}/100 | "
            f"party {self.party_power:.0f} vs challenge {self.challenge_rating:.0f} "
            f"(ratio {self.ratio:.2f}) | reward balance {self.reward_balance:.2f}"
        )


class BalanceEngine:
    """Computes how challenging-yet-fair a quest is for the current party and
    produces concrete, quantified suggestions to fix any imbalance."""

    REWARD_PER_CHALLENGE = 2.0

    def __init__(self, ideal_ratio: float = 1.0) -> None:
        self.ideal_ratio = ideal_ratio

    def party_power(self, quest: Quest, players: List[Player]) -> float:
        if not players:
            return 0.0
        base = sum(p.effective_skill(quest.quest_type) for p in players)
        return base + quest.support_power()

    def challenge_rating(self, quest: Quest) -> float:
        return quest.total_enemy_strength() + quest.total_objective_weight()

    @staticmethod
    def _tier(ratio: float) -> DifficultyTier:
        if ratio < 0.6:
            return DifficultyTier.TRIVIAL
        if ratio < 0.85:
            return DifficultyTier.EASY
        if ratio <= 1.15:
            return DifficultyTier.BALANCED
        if ratio <= 1.5:
            return DifficultyTier.HARD
        return DifficultyTier.EXTREME

    def evaluate(self, quest: Quest, players: List[Player]) -> BalanceReport:
        power = self.party_power(quest, players)
        challenge = self.challenge_rating(quest)

        if power > 0:
            ratio = challenge / power
        else:
            ratio = 5.0 if challenge > 0 else 0.0
        ratio = min(ratio, 5.0)

        tier = self._tier(ratio)
        deviation = abs(ratio - self.ideal_ratio)
        score = round(_clamp(100 - deviation * 100, 0, 100), 1)

        expected_reward = challenge * self.REWARD_PER_CHALLENGE
        actual_reward = quest.total_reward_value()
        reward_balance = round(actual_reward / expected_reward, 2) if expected_reward > 0 else 1.0

        suggestions = self._build_suggestions(quest, power, challenge, ratio, reward_balance)
        return BalanceReport(
            party_power=round(power, 1),
            challenge_rating=round(challenge, 1),
            ratio=round(ratio, 2),
            score=score,
            tier=tier,
            reward_balance=reward_balance,
            suggestions=suggestions,
        )

    def _build_suggestions(
        self,
        quest: Quest,
        power: float,
        challenge: float,
        ratio: float,
        reward_balance: float,
    ) -> List[Suggestion]:
        suggestions: List[Suggestion] = []
        target_challenge = self.ideal_ratio * power
        delta = target_challenge - challenge
        avg_strength = (
            sum(e.strength for e in quest.enemies) / len(quest.enemies)
            if quest.enemies
            else 6.0
        )

        if ratio < 0.85:  # too easy
            add = max(1, math.ceil(delta / max(avg_strength, 1.0)))
            suggestions.append(
                Suggestion(
                    "add_enemies",
                    f"Add ~{add} more enemy unit(s) to raise the challenge",
                    {"count": add},
                    f"Party power {power:.0f} outpaces challenge {challenge:.0f}; the quest is too easy.",
                )
            )
            suggestions.append(
                Suggestion(
                    "scale_enemy_strength",
                    "Increase enemy strength by 15%",
                    {"factor": 1.15},
                    "Tougher foes make each encounter more meaningful.",
                )
            )
        elif ratio > 1.15:  # too hard
            if quest.enemies:
                remove = max(1, math.floor((-delta) / max(avg_strength, 1.0)))
                suggestions.append(
                    Suggestion(
                        "remove_enemies",
                        f"Remove ~{remove} enemy unit(s) to ease the challenge",
                        {"count": remove},
                        f"Challenge {challenge:.0f} overwhelms party power {power:.0f}; the quest may be unfair.",
                    )
                )
                suggestions.append(
                    Suggestion(
                        "scale_enemy_strength",
                        "Reduce enemy strength by 15%",
                        {"factor": 0.85},
                        "Weaker foes soften the difficulty spike.",
                    )
                )
            suggestions.append(
                Suggestion(
                    "add_ally_npc",
                    "Add an ally NPC to support the party",
                    {"name": "Veteran Guard"},
                    "A helpful NPC offsets a punishing encounter.",
                )
            )

        if reward_balance < 0.75:
            suggestions.append(
                Suggestion(
                    "adjust_rewards",
                    "Increase rewards by 30% to match the risk",
                    {"factor": 1.3},
                    "Rewards are low relative to the challenge.",
                )
            )
        elif reward_balance > 1.6:
            suggestions.append(
                Suggestion(
                    "adjust_rewards",
                    "Reduce rewards by 20% to protect the game economy",
                    {"factor": 0.8},
                    "Rewards are high relative to the challenge.",
                )
            )

        if not suggestions:
            suggestions.append(
                Suggestion(
                    "info",
                    "Quest is well balanced - no changes needed.",
                    {},
                    "Difficulty and rewards are within the target range.",
                )
            )
        return suggestions


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Adaptation engine (preference-aware recommendations)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class AdaptationEngine:
    """Blends raw balance suggestions with the *aggregated preferences* of the
    players currently in the session, so changes align with what the group
    actually wants - not just a generic difficulty target."""

    def __init__(self, balance_engine: BalanceEngine) -> None:
        self.balance = balance_engine

    @staticmethod
    def _aggregate_preferences(players: List[Player]) -> Dict[str, object]:
        densities = [p.preferences.get("enemy_density") for p in players if p.preferences.get("enemy_density")]
        density = max(set(densities), key=densities.count) if densities else None

        ratios = [
            p.preferences.get("preferred_ratio")
            for p in players
            if isinstance(p.preferences.get("preferred_ratio"), (int, float))
        ]
        preferred_ratio = sum(ratios) / len(ratios) if ratios else None

        focuses = [p.preferences.get("reward_focus") for p in players if p.preferences.get("reward_focus")]
        reward_focus = max(set(focuses), key=focuses.count) if focuses else None

        return {
            "enemy_density": density,
            "preferred_ratio": preferred_ratio,
            "reward_focus": reward_focus,
        }

    def recommend(self, quest: Quest, players: List[Player]) -> List[Suggestion]:
        prefs = self._aggregate_preferences(players)

        # Temporarily bias the balance target toward the group's preference.
        original_ideal = self.balance.ideal_ratio
        if prefs["preferred_ratio"]:
            self.balance.ideal_ratio = float(prefs["preferred_ratio"])
        report = self.balance.evaluate(quest, players)
        self.balance.ideal_ratio = original_ideal

        suggestions = [s for s in report.suggestions if s.action != "info"]

        if prefs["enemy_density"] == "low" and quest.enemy_count() > 3:
            suggestions.append(
                Suggestion(
                    "consolidate_enemies",
                    "Merge weak foes into fewer elite enemies",
                    {},
                    "The group prefers fewer, stronger enemies (low density).",
                )
            )

        if prefs["reward_focus"]:
            focus = prefs["reward_focus"]
            has_focus = any(r.reward_type.value == focus for r in quest.rewards)
            if not has_focus:
                suggestions.append(
                    Suggestion(
                        "add_reward",
                        f"Add a {focus} reward the group values",
                        {"reward_type": focus},
                        "Aligns loot with the group's reward preference.",
                    )
                )

        if not suggestions:
            suggestions.append(report.suggestions[-1])  # the "info" entry
        return suggestions


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Quest simulator ("testing mode")
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class SimulationResult:
    trials: int
    success_rate: float
    avg_turns: float
    avg_party_health_pct: float
    objective_success: Dict[str, float]
    verdict: str
    party_power: float
    challenge_rating: float

    def summary(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"{self.trials} runs | success {self.success_rate:.0%} | "
            f"avg {self.avg_turns:.1f} turns | party health {self.avg_party_health_pct:.0%} "
            f"-> {self.verdict}"
        )


class QuestSimulator:
    """Monte-Carlo play-through simulator. Produces data the group can use to
    refine the quest (success rate, length, survivability, per-objective outcomes)."""

    def __init__(self, balance_engine: BalanceEngine, seed: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
        self.balance = balance_engine
        self.random = random.Random(seed)

    @staticmethod
    def _verdict(success_rate: float) -> str:
        if success_rate >= 0.85:
            return "Too easy - consider raising the difficulty"
        if success_rate >= 0.6:
            return "Balanced - challenging yet fair"
        if success_rate >= 0.4:
            return "Hard - winnable but punishing"
        return "Too hard - likely unfair"

    def simulate(self, quest: Quest, players: List[Player], trials: int = 200) -> SimulationResult:
        power = self.balance.party_power(quest, players)
        challenge = self.balance.challenge_rating(quest)
        safe_challenge = max(challenge, 1.0)

        successes = 0
        turns_total = 0.0
        health_total = 0.0
        objective_hits: Dict[str, int] = {o.description: 0 for o in quest.objectives}

        for _ in range(trials):
            effective = power * self.random.uniform(0.8, 1.2)
            trial_challenge = safe_challenge * self.random.uniform(0.85, 1.15)
            margin = effective - trial_challenge

            p_success = 1.0 / (1.0 + math.exp(-margin / (0.2 * safe_challenge + 1.0)))
            won = self.random.random() < p_success
            successes += 1 if won else 0

            turns = max(1, round(trial_challenge / max(effective, 1.0) * 6 * self.random.uniform(0.8, 1.2)))
            turns_total += turns

            if won:
                health = _clamp(0.2 + 0.8 * p_success + self.random.uniform(-0.1, 0.1), 0.0, 1.0)
            else:
                health = _clamp(0.4 * p_success, 0.0, 1.0)
            health_total += health

            for objective in quest.objectives:
                share = objective.difficulty_weight / (safe_challenge + 1.0)
                obj_p = _clamp(p_success * (1.0 - share), 0.0, 1.0)
                if self.random.random() < obj_p:
                    objective_hits[objective.description] += 1

        success_rate = successes / trials if trials else 0.0
        return SimulationResult(
            trials=trials,
            success_rate=round(success_rate, 3),
            avg_turns=round(turns_total / trials, 2) if trials else 0.0,
            avg_party_health_pct=round(health_total / trials, 3) if trials else 0.0,
            objective_success={k: round(v / trials, 3) for k, v in objective_hits.items()} if trials else {},
            verdict=self._verdict(success_rate),
            party_power=round(power, 1),
            challenge_rating=round(challenge, 1),
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Community hub (publish / rate / review / download)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class PublishedQuest:
    quest: Quest
    author: str
    description: str = ""
    reviews: List[Review] = field(default_factory=list)
    downloads: int = 0
    published_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: _new_id("pub"))

    @property
    def average_rating(self) -> float:
        if not self.reviews:
            return 0.0
        return round(sum(r.rating for r in self.reviews) / len(self.reviews), 2)


class CommunityHub:
    """A simple shared catalogue where players publish, discover, rate and
    review each other's quests."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.catalog: Dict[str, PublishedQuest] = {}

    def publish(self, quest: Quest, author: str, description: str = "") -> PublishedQuest:
        published = PublishedQuest(quest=quest.snapshot(), author=author, description=description)
        self.catalog[published.published_id] = published
        return published

    def _get(self, published_id: str) -> PublishedQuest:
        if published_id not in self.catalog:
            raise KeyError(f"No published quest with id {published_id}")
        return self.catalog[published_id]

    def review(self, published_id: str, reviewer: str, rating: int, comment: str = "") -> float:
        published = self._get(published_id)
        clamped = int(_clamp(rating, 1, 5))
        published.reviews.append(Review(reviewer=reviewer, rating=clamped, comment=comment))
        return published.average_rating

    def rate(self, published_id: str, reviewer: str, rating: int) -> float:
        return self.review(published_id, reviewer, rating, comment="")

    def download(self, published_id: str) -> Quest:
        published = self._get(published_id)
        published.downloads += 1
        return published.quest.snapshot()

    def search(
        self,
        quest_type: Optional[QuestType] = None,
        min_rating: float = 0.0,
    ) -> List[PublishedQuest]:
        results = [
            p
            for p in self.catalog.values()
            if (quest_type is None or p.quest.quest_type == quest_type)
            and p.average_rating >= min_rating
        ]
        return sorted(results, key=lambda p: (p.average_rating, len(p.reviews)), reverse=True)

    def top_quests(self, limit: int = 5) -> List[PublishedQuest]:
        return self.search()[:limit]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Collaboration session (real-time co-editing)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class CollaborationSession:
    """A live editing session for a single quest. Every edit is recorded in
    history and broadcast (with fresh balance feedback) to all participants."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        quest: Quest,
        host: Player,
        balance_engine: Optional[BalanceEngine] = None,
        adaptation_engine: Optional[AdaptationEngine] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.quest = quest
        self.host = host
        self.party: Dict[str, Player] = {}
        self.bus = EventBus()
        self.history = QuestHistory()
        self.balance = balance_engine or BalanceEngine()
        self.adaptation = adaptation_engine or AdaptationEngine(self.balance)
        self.session_id = _new_id("session")

        self.history.commit(self.quest, host.name, "Session created")
        self.join(host)

    # ---- participation ---------------------------------------------------
    @property
    def players(self) -> List[Player]:
        return list(self.party.values())

    def subscribe(self, callback: Callable[[Event], None]) -> Callable[[Event], None]:
        return self.bus.subscribe(callback)

    def join(self, player: Player) -> None:
        self.party[player.player_id] = player
        player.online = True
        self._publish("PLAYER_JOINED", player.name, f"{player.name} joined the session")
        self._broadcast_balance(player.name)

    def leave(self, player: Player) -> None:
        if player.player_id in self.party:
            del self.party[player.player_id]
            self._publish("PLAYER_LEFT", player.name, f"{player.name} left the session")

    # ---- edit operations (recorded + broadcast) -------------------------
    def add_objective(self, actor: Player, objective: Objective) -> None:
        self.quest.add_objective(objective)
        self._after_change(actor.name, f"added objective '{objective.description}'")

    def add_enemy(self, actor: Player, enemy: Enemy) -> None:
        self.quest.add_enemy(enemy)
        self._after_change(actor.name, f"added enemy {enemy.name} x{enemy.count}")

    def add_reward(self, actor: Player, reward: Reward) -> None:
        self.quest.add_reward(reward)
        self._after_change(actor.name, f"added reward {reward.name}")

    def add_npc(self, actor: Player, npc: NPC) -> None:
        npc.created_by = npc.created_by or actor.name
        self.quest.add_npc(npc)
        self._after_change(actor.name, f"added custom NPC {npc.name}")

    def add_item(self, actor: Player, item: Item) -> None:
        item.created_by = item.created_by or actor.name
        self.quest.add_item(item)
        self._after_change(actor.name, f"added custom item {item.name}")

    def set_description(self, actor: Player, description: str) -> None:
        self.quest.description = description
        self._after_change(actor.name, "updated description")

    # ---- balance / adaptation -------------------------------------------
    def evaluate(self) -> BalanceReport:
        return self.balance.evaluate(self.quest, self.players)

    def recommend(self) -> List[Suggestion]:
        return self.adaptation.recommend(self.quest, self.players)

    def apply_suggestion(self, actor: str, suggestion: Suggestion) -> None:
        action, params = suggestion.action, suggestion.params
        if action == "add_enemies":
            self.quest.increase_enemy_count(int(params.get("count", 1)))
        elif action == "remove_enemies":
            self.quest.decrease_enemy_count(int(params.get("count", 1)))
        elif action == "scale_enemy_strength":
            self.quest.scale_enemy_strength(float(params.get("factor", 1.0)))
        elif action == "adjust_rewards":
            self.quest.adjust_reward_value(float(params.get("factor", 1.0)))
        elif action == "add_ally_npc":
            self.quest.add_npc(NPC(name=str(params.get("name", "Ally")), role="ally", created_by=actor))
        elif action == "consolidate_enemies":
            self.quest.consolidate_enemies()
        elif action == "add_reward":
            rtype = RewardType(str(params.get("reward_type", "gold")))
            self.quest.add_reward(Reward(name=f"{rtype.value.title()} Cache", reward_type=rtype, value=20.0))
        elif action == "info":
            return
        self._after_change(actor, f"applied suggestion: {suggestion.description}")

    def auto_balance(self, actor: str = "AdaptationEngine", max_iterations: int = 8) -> List[Suggestion]:
        """Iteratively apply the top suggestion until the quest is balanced
        (challenge and rewards both within the target range)."""
        applied: List[Suggestion] = []
        for _ in range(max_iterations):
            report = self.evaluate()
            if report.is_balanced and 0.75 <= report.reward_balance <= 1.6:
                break
            primary = next((s for s in report.suggestions if s.action != "info"), None)
            if primary is None:
                break
            self.apply_suggestion(actor, primary)
            applied.append(primary)
        return applied

    # ---- testing mode ----------------------------------------------------
    def simulate(self, trials: int = 200, seed: Optional[int] = None) -> SimulationResult:
        return QuestSimulator(self.balance, seed=seed).simulate(self.quest, self.players, trials)

    # ---- history ---------------------------------------------------------
    def revert(self, version: int, actor: str = "system") -> int:
        new_version = self.history.revert(self.quest, version, actor)
        self._publish("REVERTED", actor, f"Reverted to v{version}")
        self._broadcast_balance(actor)
        return new_version

    # ---- community -------------------------------------------------------
    def publish_to_community(self, hub: CommunityHub, description: str = "") -> PublishedQuest:
        published = hub.publish(self.quest, self.host.name, description)
        self._publish("PUBLISHED", self.host.name, f"Published '{self.quest.title}' to the community")
        return published

    # ---- internals -------------------------------------------------------
    def _after_change(self, actor: str, message: str) -> None:
        self.history.commit(self.quest, actor, message)
        self._publish("QUEST_CHANGED", actor, message)
        self._broadcast_balance(actor)

    def _broadcast_balance(self, actor: str) -> None:
        report = self.evaluate()
        self._publish("BALANCE_FEEDBACK", "BalanceEngine", report.summary(), {"report": report})

    def _publish(self, event_type: str, actor: str, message: str, payload: Optional[Dict[str, object]] = None) -> None:
        self.bus.publish(Event(event_type, actor, message, payload or {}))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. QuestCreatorSystem (public facade)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class QuestCreatorSystem:
    """Top-level entry point: manages player accounts, sessions and the shared
    community hub."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.players: Dict[str, Player] = {}
        self.balance = BalanceEngine()
        self.adaptation = AdaptationEngine(self.balance)
        self.community = CommunityHub()
        self.sessions: Dict[str, CollaborationSession] = {}

    def register_player(
        self,
        name: str,
        combat_skill: float = 10.0,
        puzzle_skill: float = 10.0,
        exploration_skill: float = 10.0,
        preferences: Optional[Dict[str, object]] = None,
    ) -> Player:
        player = Player(
            name=name,
            combat_skill=combat_skill,
            puzzle_skill=puzzle_skill,
            exploration_skill=exploration_skill,
            preferences=preferences or {},
        )
        self.players[player.player_id] = player
        return player

    def login(self, player: Player) -> Player:
        player.online = True
        return player

    def start_session(
        self,
        title: str,
        quest_type: QuestType,
        host: Player,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> CollaborationSession:
        quest = Quest(title=title, quest_type=quest_type, description=description)
        session = CollaborationSession(quest, host, self.balance, self.adaptation)
        self.sessions[session.session_id] = session
        return session


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 12. Demonstration + self tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _print_header(title: str) -> None:
    print("\n" + "=" * 72)
    print(title)
    print("=" * 72)


def main() -> None:
    system = QuestCreatorSystem()

    _print_header("1. Players log in and start a collaborative session")
    aria = system.register_player(
        "Aria", combat_skill=14, puzzle_skill=8, exploration_skill=10,
        preferences={"preferred_ratio": 1.0, "reward_focus": "experience"},
    )
    bjorn = system.register_player(
        "Bjorn", combat_skill=18, puzzle_skill=6, exploration_skill=7,
        preferences={"enemy_density": "low"},
    )
    cleo = system.register_player(
        "Cleo", combat_skill=9, puzzle_skill=15, exploration_skill=12,
        preferences={"preferred_ratio": 1.0},
    )
    for p in (aria, bjorn, cleo):
        system.login(p)
        print(f"  logged in: {p.name} (combat={p.combat_skill}, puzzle={p.puzzle_skill}, explore={p.exploration_skill})")

    session = system.start_session("The Sunken Crypt", QuestType.COMBAT, host=aria)

    # Real-time updates: subscribe a console logger that every client would receive.
    def live_logger(event: Event) -> None:
        if event.type != "PLAYER_JOINED" or event.actor != aria.name:
            print(f"    [live] {event.type}: {event.message}")

    session.subscribe(live_logger)
    session.join(bjorn)
    session.join(cleo)

    _print_header("2. Collaborative editing with real-time balance feedback")
    aria_obj = Objective("Defeat the crypt guardian", ObjectiveType.DEFEAT, difficulty_weight=20)
    session.add_objective(aria, aria_obj)
    session.add_enemy(bjorn, Enemy("Skeleton", EnemyType.MELEE, strength=6, count=8))
    session.add_enemy(bjorn, Enemy("Crypt Guardian", EnemyType.BOSS, strength=40, count=1))
    session.add_reward(cleo, Reward("Gold Hoard", RewardType.GOLD, value=40))

    _print_header("3. Adaptive suggestions based on combined player input")
    print("  Recommendations (preference-aware):")
    for s in session.recommend():
        print(f"    * {s.description}  <- {s.rationale}")

    print("\n  Auto-balancing the quest toward a fair-yet-challenging target...")
    applied = session.auto_balance()
    for s in applied:
        print(f"    applied: {s.description}")
    print("  Result:", session.evaluate().summary())

    _print_header("4. Custom content: player-created NPC and item")
    session.add_npc(cleo, NPC("Old Hermit", role="guide", dialogue="Beware the second tomb...", created_by=cleo.name))
    session.add_item(aria, Item("Torch of Warding", "Reveals hidden traps", power=4.0, created_by=aria.name))

    _print_header("5. Testing mode: simulate the play-through")
    result = session.simulate(trials=500, seed=42)
    print("  " + result.summary())
    print("  Per-objective completion rates:")
    for desc, rate in result.objective_success.items():
        print(f"    - {desc}: {rate:.0%}")

    _print_header("6. History: review versions and revert a change")
    for line in session.history.log():
        print("  " + line)
    target_version = 3  # state shortly after the first couple of edits
    print(f"\n  Reverting to v{target_version}...")
    session.revert(target_version, actor=aria.name)
    print("  After revert:", session.evaluate().summary())

    _print_header("7. Community: publish, rate and review")
    # Re-balance after the revert, then publish.
    session.auto_balance()
    published = session.publish_to_community(system.community, description="A tense crypt delve for 3 heroes.")
    system.community.review(published.published_id, "Bjorn", 5, "Loved the boss fight!")
    system.community.review(published.published_id, "Cleo", 4, "Fair and fun.")
    system.community.rate(published.published_id, "Guest42", 5)
    print(f"  Published '{published.quest.title}' (avg rating {published.average_rating}/5, {len(published.reviews)} reviews)")
    print("  Community top quests:")
    for pub in system.community.top_quests():
        print(f"    - {pub.quest.title} by {pub.author}: {pub.average_rating}/5 ({pub.quest.quest_type.value})")

    _print_header("Final quest")
    downloaded = system.community.download(published.published_id)
    print(downloaded.describe())


def run_self_tests() -> None:
    """Lightweight assertions that exercise every major capability."""
    system = QuestCreatorSystem()
    p1 = system.register_player("Tester1", combat_skill=12)
    p2 = system.register_player("Tester2", combat_skill=12)
    session = system.start_session("Test Quest", QuestType.COMBAT, host=p1)
    session.join(p2)

    # Real-time events captured by a subscriber.
    received: List[Event] = []
    session.subscribe(received.append)

    # Edits produce events + history versions.
    base_versions = session.history.current_version
    session.add_enemy(p1, Enemy("Ogre", EnemyType.MELEE, strength=30, count=4))  # deliberately too hard
    assert session.history.current_version == base_versions + 1
    assert any(e.type == "BALANCE_FEEDBACK" for e in received), "should broadcast live feedback"

    hard = session.evaluate()
    assert hard.tier in (DifficultyTier.HARD, DifficultyTier.EXTREME), hard.tier

    # Auto-balance improves the balance score and never makes it worse.
    before_score = session.evaluate().score
    session.auto_balance()
    after = session.evaluate()
    assert after.score >= before_score, (before_score, after.score)

    # History + revert restores prior state exactly.
    session.add_reward(p1, Reward("Bonus", RewardType.GOLD, value=15))
    version_with_reward = session.history.current_version
    reward_count = len(session.quest.rewards)
    session.add_reward(p1, Reward("Extra", RewardType.GOLD, value=15))
    assert len(session.quest.rewards) == reward_count + 1
    session.revert(version_with_reward)
    assert len(session.quest.rewards) == reward_count, "revert should restore reward list"

    # Simulation returns sane, bounded data.
    result = session.simulate(trials=300, seed=7)
    assert result.trials == 300
    assert 0.0 <= result.success_rate <= 1.0
    assert 0.0 <= result.avg_party_health_pct <= 1.0
    assert result.avg_turns >= 1.0

    # Custom content attribution.
    session.add_npc(p1, NPC("Sage"))
    session.add_item(p2, Item("Map", power=2.0))
    assert session.quest.npcs[-1].created_by == p1.name
    assert session.quest.items[-1].created_by == p2.name

    # Community publish / rate / review with clamping.
    pub = session.publish_to_community(system.community)
    system.community.rate(pub.published_id, "A", 5)
    system.community.rate(pub.published_id, "B", 3)
    system.community.rate(pub.published_id, "C", 99)  # clamped to 5
    assert pub.average_rating == round((5 + 3 + 5) / 3, 2), pub.average_rating
    assert len(system.community.search(quest_type=QuestType.COMBAT)) == 1

    # Different quest types are supported.
    puzzle_session = system.start_session("Riddle Room", QuestType.PUZZLE, host=p1)
    puzzle_session.add_objective(p1, Objective("Solve the rune lock", ObjectiveType.SOLVE, difficulty_weight=10))
    assert puzzle_session.quest.quest_type == QuestType.PUZZLE

    print("All self-tests passed.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_self_tests()
    main()
